Description.653: Twist angle at each section (positive decreases angle of attack)

Description.732: 1D array of the relative thicknesses of the blade defined along span.
Description.733: 1D array of the chord values defined along blade span.
Description.734: 1D array of the chord values defined along blade span.
Description.735: 1D array of the airfoil position relative to the reference axis, specifying the distance in meters along the chordline from the reference axis to the leading edge. 0 means that the airfoil is pinned at the leading edge, a positive offset means that the leading edge is upstream of the reference axis in local chordline coordinates, and a negative offset that the leading edge aft of the reference axis.
Description.736: 1D array of the twist values defined along blade span. The twist is defined positive for negative rotations around the z axis (the same as in BeamDyn).
Description.737: 3D array of the non-dimensional x and y airfoil coordinates of the airfoils interpolated along span for n_span stations. The leading edge is place at x=0 and y=0.
Description.738: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the blade reference axis, defined along blade span. The coordinate system is the one of BeamDyn: it is placed at blade root with x pointing the suction side of the blade, y pointing the trailing edge and z along the blade span. A standard configuration will have negative x values (prebend), if swept positive y values, and positive z values.
Description.74: Number of assembly lines used when assembly occurs at the port.
Description.742: 1D array of the dimensional spanwise grid defined along the rotor (hub radius to blade tip projected on the plane)
Description.743: Diameter of the wind turbine rotor specified by the user, defined as 2 x (Rhub + blade length along z) * cos(precone).
Description.746: Cut out wind speed. This is the wind speed where region III ends.
Description.747: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the blade reference axis, defined along blade span. The coordinate system is the one of BeamDyn: it is placed at blade root with x pointing the suction side of the blade, y pointing the trailing edge and z along the blade span. A standard configuration will have negative x values (prebend), if swept positive y values, and positive z values.
Description.748: Diameter of the rotor specified by the user, defined as 2 x (Rhub + blade length along z) * cos(precone).
Description.749: Radius of the hub. It defines the distance of the blade root from the rotor center along the coned line.

Description.752: Number of blades of the rotor.

Description.820: Index used to fix a layer to another
Description.821: Index used to fix a layer to another
Description.822: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the tower reference axis. The coordinate system is the global coordinate system of OpenFAST: it is placed at tower base with x pointing downwind, y pointing on the side and z pointing vertically upwards. A standard tower configuration will have zero x and y values and positive z values.
Description.823: Vertical distance from tower top to hub center.
Description.824: Height of the hub specified by the user.
Description.825: Scalar of the rotor diameter, defined as 2 x (Rhub + blade length along z) * cos(precone).
Description.827: 1D array of the density of the fibers of the materials.
Description.828: 1D array of the density of the materials. For composites, this is the density of the laminate.
Description.829: 1D array of the dry aerial density of the composite fabrics. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.830: 1D array of the ply thicknesses of the materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.831: 1D array of the non-dimensional fiber volume fraction of the composite materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.832: 1D array of the non-dimensional fiber weight- fraction of the composite materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.834: 1D array of flags to set whether a material is isotropic (0) or orthtropic (1). Each entry represents a material.
Description.835: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the tower reference axis. The coordinate system is the global coordinate system of OpenFAST: it is placed at tower base with x pointing downwind, y pointing on the side and z pointing vertically upwards. A standard tower configuration will have zero x and y values and positive z values.
Description.836: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the tower reference axis. The coordinate system is the global coordinate system of OpenFAST: it is placed at tower base with x pointing downwind, y pointing on the side and z pointing vertically upwards. A standard tower configuration will have zero x and y values and positive z values.
Description.88: Number of years to simulation the operations and maintenance phase of the farm lifecycle
Description.89: Distance, in km, that servicing equipment must travel daily to reach the wind farm
Description.90: Distance, in km, that tugboats must travel to reach the wind farm for tow-to-port repairs
Description.91: Reduced speed applied to servicing equipment in the reduced speed period
Description.93: Turbine CapEx per kW of nameplate capacity
Description.96: Number of hours to complete the repair
Description.97: Total cost of materials used to complete the repair. If between 0 and 1, the cost is proportional to the turbine CapEx.
Description.99: Number of hours to complete the repair
